title: xyris asperula
intro: Xyris asperula is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae, commonly known as the yellow-eyed grass. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can provide health benefits such as wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions. Xyris asperula can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
